proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +40.7pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
D-8 review of R-multiple distribution (Day-6 forced-exit dominance) remains open; if resolved unfavorably, cohort R could drift further from Tier S threshold
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +40.7pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +40.7pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 6), so the cohort is dormant and the flagged 54.33% Choppy concentration on 127 open positions is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-26) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.8pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime stability is 'shifting' — Bear may or may not re-emerge soon; cohort remains dormant until it does
R-multiple 0.98 under D-8 review per project anchor — Day-6 forced-exit dominance could erode realized edge
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +30.8pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +30.8pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 6), so the cohort is dormant and the Choppy-skewed portfolio concentration (54.33%, below 80% trigger) is not compounded; a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 06-26) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (96.1%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 0.85; require any new adds to be DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY in Bear/Bull regime to rebalance directionality
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind the 96% long-heavy book simultaneously with no statistical justification from cohort_health, producing correlated drawdown across Bank/FMCG/Auto/CapGoods clusters where UP_TRI×Choppy exposure compounds without validated edge.

evidence: Book is overwhelmingly directional long (UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 124/127 = 97.6%)
Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort only at Candidate tier (n=256) — unvalidated edge
Top cell UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank at 14.17% (18/127) — within cell tolerance but adds clustering
Regime split Choppy 54.3% / Bull 37.0% / Bear 8.7% — long-biased regimes dominate
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 0.85; require any new adds to be DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY in Bear/Bull regime to rebalance directionality
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind the 96% long-heavy book simultaneously with no statistical justification from cohort_health, producing correlated drawdown across Bank/FMCG/Auto/CapGoods clusters where UP_TRI×Choppy exposure compounds without validated edge.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 122/127 = 96.1% (above 0.85 threshold) and regime_watch context shows the book remains in unstable Choppy/Bull mix without validated cohort backing — matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=256). While regime concentration (Choppy 54.3%) and the top cell (UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank at 14.17%) are within tolerance, the signal-type monoculture on an unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
